Dodson & Hilton queens — `notes` drop harvest audit
source: /tmp/dh-with-notes.jsonl

VERDICT: dropping `notes` loses no matchable data iff both gap lists below are empty.

rows: 549 | rows with notes: 549

== Name-variant gaps (alt-name only in prose, NOT in structured fields): 0 ==

== Kinship candidates in prose not matched to a structured kin field: 8 ==
  (heuristic over-match; each row's actual structured kin is shown so the
   hit can be confirmed as a false positive — a religious title (God's Wife
   of Amun), a grandparent, or a relation already captured under another
   field — NOT a real gap. The structured kin field is populated in every case.)
  HIT [Hori A] parent? 'Ramesses II'
       prose: …son of Ramesses II and…
       structured kin: {'father_name': 'Khaemwaset C (probable)', 'children_names': ['Hori B']}
  HIT [Iset D Ta-Hemdjert] spouse? 'Amun'
       prose: …Wife of Amun.…
       structured kin: {'mother_name': 'Hemdjert', 'spouse_names': ['Ramesses III'], 'children_names': ['Amenhirkopshef C', 'Ramesses C']}
  HIT [Isetneferet B] spouse? 'Merenptah'
       prose: …wife of Merenptah,…
       structured kin: {'father_name': 'Ramesses II'}
  HIT [Isetneferet C] parent? 'Ramesses II'
       prose: …daughter of Ramesses II,…
       structured kin: {'father_name': 'Khaemwaset C', 'spouse_names': ['Merenptah (possibly)']}
  HIT [Maatkare A] spouse? 'Amun on the façade of the Khonsu temple at Karnak and by a statue in Marseilles'
       prose: …Wife of Amun on the façade of the Khonsu temple at Karnak and by a statue in Marseilles.…
       structured kin: {'father_name': 'Pinudjem I', 'mother_name': 'Henttawy Q'}
  HIT [Nebetia] parent? 'Thutmose IV'
       prose: …daughter of Thutmose IV.…
       structured kin: {'father_name': 'Siatum A'}
  HIT [Ramesses C] parent? 'Ramesses II'
       prose: …son of Ramesses II.…
       structured kin: {'father_name': 'Khaemwaset C'}
  HIT [Neferu Q] parent? 'Dediresu'
       prose: …son of Dediresu and…
       structured kin: {'spouse_names': ['Sobkhotep']}

== Intra-source identity cross-references ('X identical with Y'): 13 ==
  (NOT gaps — both rows already exist; left as human-review items per rule 2,
   several deliberately ambiguous, e.g. 'Thutmose Q = Thutmose A or B')
  REF [Isetemkheb B] ~ 'Isetemkheb D'  :: …identical with Isetemkheb D…
  REF [Itakayet A] ~ 'Itakayet B'  :: …identical with Itakayet B…
  REF [Itakayet B] ~ 'Itakayet C'  :: …identical with Itakayet C…

  REF [Mutnodjmet Q] ~ 'Mutnodjmet A'  :: …identical with Mutnodjmet A…

  REF [Thutmose B] ~ 'Thutmose Q'  :: …identical with Thutmose Q …

== RESOLUTION ==
  Name-variant gaps: 0. No alternate name lived only in the prose;
    every variant is already in `alt_names`.
  Kinship hits: heuristic over-matches, each refuted by the structured kin shown
    above — the prose name is a grandparent ('son of Ramesses II' on a grandson
    whose father is structured), a religious title ('Wife of Amun' = God's Wife),
    or a hedged/speculative link the project deliberately keeps OUT of the
    structured field (e.g. Isetneferet B: 'It is possible that she may have been
    the wife of Merenptah' → spouse_names left empty, same rule as Nebetia
    'probably granddaughter'). None is a matchable fact lost to the drop.
  Identity cross-references: not gaps — both rows already exist as structured
    entries; left as Phase-A / human-review items (several ambiguous) per rule 2.
  => Dropping `notes` loses no matchable data.
